title: The Weekly EVA Panel
wikitext:
[]: The Weekly EVA Panel             Quote Post by Mighty Gojira » Mon Jan 24, 2011 12:23 am Here's the premise. Each week I will ask if anyone would like to fill one of five "seats" in an online panel. The seats will be filled by anyone wanting to represent one style of Evangelion fan, but questions won't necessarily be about that section of fandom. So this week, I was wondering if anyone would like to fill these seats. It's first come, first serve of course. -Originally saw NGE as it was released, appropriate by your country of origin. -New Fan. Saw Rebuild first. -Someone who prefers the story-line plot. That is to say, the external conflicts of the show. Angel fights, super-science, conspiracies, etc. Y'know, the fun stuff. -Someone who prefers the character based drama of NGE. Enjoys the internal conflicts. Perhaps sees the plot as more of trappings for a character drama show. -This week's wild card: Someone who has more of a background as an American animation fan. Better seated by someone who has an appreciation and knowledge of the old "Hollywood-era" (Warners and Disney primarily). Well, thanks in advance. I'll ask my first question of the panel if the seats get filled.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Mon Jan 24, 2011 8:59 am Mighty Gojira wrote: -Originally saw NGE as it was released, appropriate by your country of origin.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 24, 2011 9:24 am Whatever became of the last podcast people tried to do?

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Mon Jan 24, 2011 9:29 am I don't think that's supposed to be a podcast.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Jan 24, 2011 9:37 am I'm not sure what your schedule is, but I think I can take "Someone who prefers the character based drama of NGE." Are you thinking in terms of one question a day or - ?

[]: Re: The Weekly EVA Panel             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jan 24, 2011 9:45 am Mighty Gojira wrote: -Someone who prefers the story-line plot. That is to say, the external conflicts of the show. Angel fights, super-science, conspiracies, etc. Y'know, the fun stuff.

[]: Re: The Weekly EVA Panel             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon Jan 24, 2011 11:08 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I nominate C.A.P. for this role here. Though I'm knowledgeable in the old Hollywood-era, my expertise is in live-action film. C.A.P. would be perfect for the classic American animations.

[]:             Quote Post by Mighty Gojira » Mon Jan 24, 2011 1:12 pm Freaky's suggestions regarding nomenclature will be duly noted. Questions will be daily. I may interject during posts to respond to answers that may need additional clarification. I'll generally keep my opinions to myself. It wouldn't be appropriate for the "host" to sound off after-all. If seats get filled by the time I'm off work tonight, I'll post the introduction to this week's panel, and also lead with the first question.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Jan 24, 2011 9:24 pm Sounds fun, I can definitely do that. Come on, people! Surely there are others who want in on this.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 24, 2011 9:32 pm Warren Peace wrote: Surely there are others who want in on this.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Jan 24, 2011 10:10 pm I'm interested in what Gojira does with it. Might not amount to much, who knows?
